data.content:
Now I have the full picture. I see version conflicts:
- **System.Text.Json**: 10.0.1 vs 8.0.4 → use **10.0.1**
- **Azure.Identity**: 1.13.2 vs 1.11.4 → use **1.13.2**

Let me create `Directory.Packages.props` and strip versions from all `.csproj` files.

arguments.file_text:
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageVersion Include="Azure.Identity" Version="1.13.2" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting" Version="1.15.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="10.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.reasoningText:
Now I have all the information. Let me analyze the packages and their versions:

| Package | Api | Worker | Shared | Tests |
|---------|-----|--------|--------|-------|
| System.Text.Json | 10.0.1 | 8.0.4 | 10.0.1 | 10.0.1 |
| Azure.Identity | 1.13.2 | - | 1.11.4 | - |
| Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting | - | 8.0.1 | - | - |
| OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting | - | 1.15.0 | - | - |


| xunit | - | - | - | 2.9.3 |

I've identified the version conflicts: System.Text.Json has 10.0.1 and 8.0.4 across projects, and Azure.Identity has 1.13.2 versus 1.11.4. I'll resolve these by using the higher versions. To implement centralized package management, I need to create a Directory.Packages.props file at the solution root with the central version management enabled and package version entries for all dependencies, then remove the individual version specifications from each project file.

arguments.file_text:
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageVersion Include="Azure.Identity" Version="1.13.2" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting" Version="1.15.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="10.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

**Created** `Directory.Packages.props` with centralized versions:
| Package | Version (highest chosen) |
|---|---|
| Azure.Identity | 1.13.2 (was also 1.11.4) |

| xunit | 2.9.3 |

**Updated** all 4 `.csproj` files — removed `Version` attributes from every `<PackageReference>`. Two conflicts were resolved by picking the highest version: **System.Text.Json** (10.0.1 over 8.0.4) and **Azure.Identity** (1.13.2 over 1.11.4).
